responses.rooftop_addition: Rooftop addition (location of bedroom or living room)
responses.a4_to_door: More than A4 paper and less than a standard door

HALST_A1a.question: What type of building is this residence?
HALST_A1a.explanation: Building type affects structural safety and environmental health risks

HALST_A1b.question: What part of the building does the respondent occupy?
HALST_A1b.explanation: Occupancy density affects privacy, noise exposure, and infection transmission risk

HALST_A2a.question: How many floors does this building have aboveground (inclusive of rooftop additions)?
HALST_A2a.explanation: Building height affects evacuation risks and structural stability

HALST_A2b.question: Does this building have any basement floors (including a parking garage)?
HALST_A2b.explanation: Basements can have moisture and air quality issues

HALST_A3a.question: On which floor of the building is the participant's bedroom? (if the participant sleeps in the living room, consider it as their bedroom)
HALST_A3a.explanation: Floor level affects evacuation access and air quality exposure

HALST_A3b.question: Does this home have a living room? (answer 'no' if the participant sleeps in the living room)
HALST_A3b.explanation: Lack of separate living space indicates overcrowding and reduced quality of life

HALST_A3e.question: What type of doorway does the bedroom have? (if the living room is considered the bedroom, this describes the doorway to the kitchen)
HALST_A3e.explanation: Privacy and sound control affect sleep quality and mental well-being

HALST_A4a.question: Does this home have a kitchen? (cooking appliances inside the bedroom does not qualify)
HALST_A4a.explanation: Lack of proper kitchen facilities affects nutrition and food safety

HALST_A5.question: Is there a bathroom inside the participant's bedroom? (if 'yes' inspect this bathroom in the later section, if 'no' inspect the bathroom in which the participant bathes)
HALST_A5.explanation: Bathroom accessibility affects convenience and hygiene

HALST_B1.question: What is the level of mold odor in the bedroom? (evaluate upon entering the room)
HALST_B1.explanation: Mold odor indicates potential respiratory health hazards and moisture problems

HALST_B2.question: What material is the bedroom ceiling composed of?
HALST_B2.explanation: Ceiling materials affect air quality and potential hazardous material exposure

HALST_B3.question: What material is the bedroom walls composed of?
HALST_B3.explanation: Wall materials can harbor allergens and affect indoor air quality

HALST_B4.question: What material is the bedroom floor composed of?
HALST_B4.explanation: Floor materials affect allergen accumulation and cleaning difficulty

HALST_B5.question: What is the combined area of water stains?
HALST_B5.explanation: Water stains indicate moisture problems that can lead to mold growth

HALST_B6a.question: What is the combined area of visible mold?
HALST_B6a.explanation: Visible mold poses serious respiratory health risks and indicates moisture problems

HALST_B7.question: What is the combined area of wall cancer (bì ái)?
HALST_B7.explanation: Wall deterioration indicates structural moisture problems and potential health hazards

HALST_B8.question: Are there any walls that could not be fully inspected?
HALST_B8.explanation: Incomplete inspection may miss important environmental health hazards

HALST_B9.question: How many potted plants are in the bedroom?
HALST_B9.explanation: Excessive plants can increase humidity and allergen exposure

HALST_B10a.question: Is the mattress placed directly on the floor?
HALST_B10a.explanation: Floor-placed mattresses have poor air circulation and increased dust mite exposure

HALST_E2a.question: Is there a balcony door inside this room? (if the living room is considered to be the bedroom, this includes any exterior door)
HALST_E2a.explanation: Lack of exterior access affects ventilation and emergency egress

HALST_E2c.question: In the past week, when did you normally open the balcony door or balcony door window?
HALST_E2c.explanation: Ventilation patterns affect indoor air quality and pollutant exposure

HALST_E3a.question: Does this room have any exterior windows?
HALST_E3a.explanation: Lack of exterior windows severely limits natural ventilation and light

HALST_E3c.question: In the past week, when did you normally open the window?
HALST_E3c.explanation: Window usage patterns directly affect indoor air quality and ventilation

HALST_E10.question: In the past week, how many people regularly slept in this room?
HALST_E10.explanation: Bedroom overcrowding affects air quality, disease transmission, and sleep quality

HALST_E14a.question: In the past week, did you cook food or boil water in the bedroom?
HALST_E14a.explanation: Cooking in bedroom creates serious fire, air quality, and safety hazards

HALST_D1.question: How old is this building?
HALST_D1.explanation: Building age affects structural integrity, materials safety, and maintenance needs

HALST_D2.question: How many people reside in this home?
HALST_D2.explanation: Household density affects overcrowding risk and disease transmission potential

HALST_D3.question: Do you have any indoor pets? (excluding fish)
HALST_D3.explanation: Indoor pets can trigger allergies and affect air quality for sensitive individuals

HALST_D4a.question: In the past week, did you burn incense indoors?
HALST_D4a.explanation: Indoor incense burning contributes to particulate matter and air pollution

HALST_D5a.question: In the past week, were there any sources of smoke indoors apart from incense burning and cooking (such as cigarette smoking)?
HALST_D5a.explanation: Indoor tobacco smoking creates severe health hazards for all occupants

HALST_D6a.question: Does this home have an indoor gas water heater?
HALST_D6a.explanation: Indoor gas water heaters can produce combustion byproducts if not properly ventilated

HALST_D7.question: Does this home have a functional smoke detector?
HALST_D7.explanation: Functional smoke detectors are essential for fire safety and survival

HALST_D8.question: Does this home have a functional carbon monoxide detector?
HALST_D8.explanation: Carbon monoxide detectors prevent deadly poisoning from gas appliances

HALST_I1a.question: Are rugs and floor mats without rolled-up edges?
HALST_I1a.explanation: Rolled-up rug edges create serious trip and fall hazards

HALST_I1c.question: Are all cords and furniture clear of walkways and neatly stowed away to avoid tripping?
HALST_I1c.explanation: Cords and furniture in walkways create dangerous trip hazards

HALST_I2a.question: Are the indoor lights bright?
HALST_I2a.explanation: Poor lighting increases fall risk and affects daily activities

HALST_I6a.question: Are the floors cleared of clutter?
HALST_I6a.explanation: Floor clutter creates major trip hazards and prevents safe navigation

HALST_G3.question: In the past week, how often was the range hood used when cooking with the gas stove?
HALST_G3.explanation: Range hood usage prevents indoor air pollution from cooking combustion

HALST_G4.question: In the past week, when did you normally open exterior windows or doors?
HALST_G4.explanation: Kitchen ventilation during cooking reduces indoor pollutant accumulation

HALST_H1.question: What is the combined area of visible mold?
HALST_H1.explanation: Bathroom mold indicates moisture problems and poses respiratory health risks

HALST_H2.question: In the past week, when did you normally open the bathroom window?
HALST_H2.explanation: Bathroom ventilation prevents moisture buildup and mold growth

HALST_I4a.question: Does the bathroom floor have anti-slip protection?
HALST_I4a.explanation: Bathroom floors without anti-slip protection pose serious fall risks

HALST_I4b.question: Are there grab bars installed next to the shower or toilet?
HALST_I4b.explanation: Grab bars are essential bathroom safety features for preventing falls
